prerequisites: No account, API key, or credentials are required: this connector reads from Yahoo Finance's public price chart API, which is unauthenticated.
Outbound HTTPS access to query1.finance.yahoo.com (the v8/finance/chart endpoint) from the machine running pm.
One or more valid Yahoo Finance ticker symbols (for example AAPL, MSFT, BTC-USD) to supply in the required 'tickers' config field.
Optionally, the candle granularity and lookback window you want, supplied via the 'interval' and 'range' config fields.

authMethods.name: None (public API)
authMethods.summary: The connector's descriptor declares no secret fields, and the underlying Yahoo Finance chart endpoint (query1.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart) is publicly reachable without any token, key, or login. There is nothing to authenticate; configure the data fields and run.

setupSteps.title: Choose the tickers to pull
setupSteps.body: Set the required 'tickers' config field to the Yahoo Finance symbol(s) you want price history for (for example AAPL for Apple, MSFT for Microsoft, or BTC-USD for Bitcoin in USD). This is the only required field.

setupSteps.title: Set the candle interval (optional)
setupSteps.body: Use the optional 'interval' config field to pick the granularity of each data point, for example 1d for daily candles. Finer intraday intervals are only valid for short lookback ranges.

setupSteps.title: Set the lookback range (optional)
setupSteps.body: Use the optional 'range' config field to choose how far back to pull, for example 1d, 5d, 1mo, 1y, or max. Leave it unset to use the connector's default.

setupSteps.title: Run the sync
setupSteps.body: No credentials step is needed. With 'tickers' set (and 'interval'/'range' adjusted if desired), run the connector; pm requests price history directly from the public Yahoo Finance chart API over HTTPS.

sources.title: Yahoo Finance v8 finance chart endpoint (AAPL, interval and range query parameters)
